signals.raw:
Nasdaq Round-the-Clock Trading by June 30? | NO | HIGH confidence**  
Polymarket's top trending market shows "Nasdaq round-the-clock trading by June 30?" at 22% YES odds with $43.9K volume, signaling traders' strong skepticism (78% NO implied) amid low liquidity ($1.5K); no whale activity or X posts noted in real-time data[1].

signals.headline: Q4 2025 GDP Final Estimate Revised Down to 0
signals.raw:
Q4 2025 GDP Final Estimate Revised Down to 0.5% | YES | HIGH**  
The final Q4 2025 GDP estimate was revised to 0.5% annualized from prior figures, with full-year 2025 GDP at 2.1% and Q1 2026 tracking 1.3%, signaling softer growth amid weaker investment and shutdown effects.[2][1]

signals.headline: March 2026 CPI Jumps 0
signals.raw:
March 2026 CPI Jumps 0.9% MoM (3.3% YoY) | YES | HIGH**  
Consumer prices rose 0.9% month-over-month—the largest since mid-2022—driven by energy surges from the Iran war (gasoline 71% of increase), with core CPI at 0.2% MoM and 2.6% YoY; YoY rate hit highest since May 2024.[2]

signals.headline: Weekly Jobless Claims Rise to 219K (Apr 4 Week) | NO | MED**
signals.raw:
Weekly Jobless Claims Rise to 219K (Apr 4 Week) | NO | MED**  
Initial claims increased modestly to 219,000 due to holiday volatility, but continuing claims fell to 1.794M (lowest since May 2024), affirming labor market stability near 4% unemployment.[2][1]

signals.headline: IMF Projects 2026 GDP at 2
signals.raw:
IMF Projects 2026 GDP at 2.4%, Core PCE Inflation to 2% by H1 2027 | YES | MED**  
IMF's 2026 Article IV consultation forecasts GDP growth rising to 2.4%, tariff effects fading, core PCE inflation returning to 2% with lower oil prices, and unemployment near 4%.[1]
